subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: Shows help

subcommands.name: addon
subcommands.description: Addons management commands: install, remove (fin help addon)

subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: Install addon

args.name: Name
args.description: See available addons in the Addons Repository https://github.com/docksal/addons

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Remove addon

subcommands.name: alias
subcommands.description: Manage aliases that allow fin @alias execution (fin help alias). Create/update alias with <alias_name> that links to <path>

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Show aliases list

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Remove alias

subcommands.name: db
subcommands.description: Manage databases (fin help db)

options.name: --db
options.description: Use another database (default is the one set with 'MYSQL_DATABASE')

options.name: --db-user
options.description: Use another mysql username (default is 'root')

options.name: --db-password
options.description: Use another database password (default is the one set with 'MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD', see fin config)

options.name: --db-charset
options.description: Override charset when creating a database (default is utf8)

options.name: --db-collation
options.description: Override collation when creating a database (default is utf8_general_ci)

subcommands.name: import
subcommands.description: Truncate the database and import from SQL dump file or stdin

options.name: --progress
options.description: Show import progess (requires pv)

options.name: --no-truncate
options.description: Do no truncate database before import

subcommands.name: dump
subcommands.description: Dump a database into an SQL dump file or stdout

name: list
ls
subcommands.description: Show list of existing databases

subcommands.name: cli
subcommands.description: Open command line interface to the DB server (and execute query if provided)

subcommands.name: create
subcommands.description: Create a database

subcommands.name: drop
subcommands.description: Delete a database

subcommands.name: truncate
subcommands.description: Truncate a database (defaults to the `default`)

subcommands.name: hosts
subcommands.description: Hosts file commands: add, remove, list (fin help hosts)

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Add hostname to hosts file. If none provided uses VIRTUAL_HOST

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Remove lines containing hostname from hosts file. If none provided uses VIRTUAL_HOST

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Output hosts file

subcommands.name: project
subcommands.description: Manage project(s) (fin help project)

subcommands.name: start
subcommands.description: Start project services (alias: fin start)

subcommands.name: up
subcommands.description: Configuration re-read and start project services (alias: fin up)

subcommands.name: stop
subcommands.description: Stop all or specified project services (alias: fin stop)

name: --all
-a
options.description: Stop all services on all Docksal projects

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: List project services (alias: fin ps)

subcommands.name: restart
subcommands.description: Restart project services (alias: fin restart)

subcommands.name: reset
subcommands.description: Recreate all or specified project services, their containers and volumes

name: remove
rm
subcommands.description: Remove all project services, networks and all their volumes, or specified services only

name: --force
-f
options.description: Do not ask for confirmation when deleting all project services

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List running Docksal projects (alias: fin pl)

name: --all
-a
options.description: List all Docksal projects (stopped as well)

subcommands.name: create
subcommands.description: Create a new project with a pre-configured boilerplate: Drupal, Wordpress, Magento, Laravel, Backdrop, Hugo, Gatsby, and others

options.name: --name
options.description: Provide project name upfront

options.name: --choice
options.description: Provide software choice number upfront

options.name: --repo
options.description: Clone from a custom repo: name (--choice is set to '0' automatically)

options.name: --branch
options.description: Clone from a custom repo: branch name (optional)

name: --yes
-y
options.description: Avoid confirmation

subcommands.name: config
subcommands.description: Show project configuration

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: Build or rebuild services (alias for 'docker-compose build')

subcommands.name: ssh-key
subcommands.description: Manage SSH keys (fin help ssh-key)

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Add a private SSH key from $HOME/.ssh by file name. Adds all default keys (id_rsa/id_dsa/id_ecdsa/id_ed25519) if no file name is given

options.name: --quiet
options.description: Suppress key already loaded notifications

subcommands.name: ls
subcommands.description: List SSH keys loaded in the docksal-ssh-agent

subcommands.name: rm
subcommands.description: Remove all keys from the docksal-ssh-agent

subcommands.name: new
subcommands.description: Generate a new SSH key pair

subcommands.name: system
subcommands.description: Manage Docksal state (fin help system)

subcommands.name: reset
subcommands.description: Reset Docksal

subcommands.name: start
subcommands.description: Start Docksal

subcommands.name: stop
subcommands.description: Stop Docksal

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Check Docksal status

subcommands.name: vm
subcommands.description: Manage Docksal VM (fin help vm)

subcommands.name: start
subcommands.description: Start the machine (create if needed)

subcommands.name: stop
subcommands.description: Stop the machine

subcommands.name: kill
subcommands.description: Forcibly stop the machine

subcommands.name: restart
subcommands.description: Restart the machine

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Get the status

subcommands.name: ssh
subcommands.description: Log into ssh or run a command via ssh

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Remove Docksal machine and cleanup after it

subcommands.name: ip
subcommands.description: Show the machine IP address

subcommands.name: ls
subcommands.description: List all docker machines

subcommands.name: env
subcommands.description: Display the commands to set up the shell for direct use of Docker client

subcommands.name: mount
subcommands.description: Try remounting host filesystem (NFS on macOS, SMB on Windows)

subcommands.name: ram
subcommands.description: Show memory size

args.name: megabyte
args.description: Set memory size. Default is 1024 (requires vm restart)

subcommands.name: hdd
subcommands.description: Show disk size and usage

subcommands.name: stats
subcommands.description: Show CPU and network usage

subcommands.name: regenerate-certs
subcommands.description: Regenerate TLS certificates and restart the VM

subcommands.name: bash
subcommands.description: Open shell into service's container. Defaults to cli

subcommands.name: config
subcommands.description: Show or change configuration (fin help config)

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Display configuration for the current project

options.name: --show-secrets
options.description: Do not truncate value of SECRET_* environment vars

subcommands.name: env
subcommands.description: Display only environment variables section

subcommands.name: yml
subcommands.description: Display static YML project config suitable for export (NOTE: SECRET_* values will not be hidden)

subcommands.name: generate
subcommands.description: Generate empty Docksal configuration for the project

options.name: --stack
options.description: Set non-default DOCKSAL_STACK during config generate

options.name: --docroot
options.description: Set non-default DOCROOT during config generate

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Set value(s) for the variable(s) in project ENV file

options.name: --global
options.description: Set for global ENV file

options.name: --env
options.description: Set in environment specific project ENV file

name: remove
rm
subcommands.description: Remove variable(s) from project ENV file

options.name: --global
options.description: Remove from global ENV file

options.name: --env
options.description: Remove from environment specific project ENV file

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: Get the value of the single variable from project ENV file

options.name: --global
options.description: Get value from global ENV file

options.name: --env
options.description: Get value from environment specific project ENV file

subcommands.name: exec
subcommands.description: Execute a command or a script in cli

subcommands.name: exec-url
subcommands.description: Download script from URL and run it on host (URL should be public)

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Initialize a project (override it with your own automation, see fin help init)

subcommands.name: image
subcommands.description: Image management commands: registry, save, load (fin help image)

subcommands.name: registry
subcommands.description: Show all Docksal images on Docker Hub

args.name: image name
args.description: Show all tags for a certain image

subcommands.name: save
subcommands.description: Save docker images into a tar archive

options.name: --system
options.description: Save Docksal system images

options.name: --project
options.description: Save current project's images

options.name: --all
options.description: Save all images available on the host

subcommands.name: load
subcommands.description: Load docker images from a tar archive

subcommands.name: logs
subcommands.description: Show service logs (e.g., Apache logs, MySQL logs) and Unison logs (fin help logs)

name: -f
--follow
options.description: Follow log output

options.name: --no-color
options.description: Produce monochrome output

options.name: --no-log-prefix
options.description: Don't print prefix in logs

options.name: --since
options.description: Show logs since timestamp (e.g. 2013-01-02T13:23:37Z) or relative (e.g. 42m for 42 minutes)

options.name: --tail
options.description: Number of lines to show from the end of the logs for each container. (default "all")

name: -t
--timestamps
options.description: Show timestamps

options.name: --until
options.description: Show logs before a timestamp (e.g. 2013-01-02T13:23:37Z) or relative (e.g. 42m for 42 minutes)

name: run-cli
rc
subcommands.description: Run a command in a standalone cli container in the current directory (fin help run-cli)

options.name: --clean
options.description: Run command with a non-persistent $HOME directory

options.name: --clenup
options.description: Clean the persistent $HOME directory and run command

options.name: --debug
options.description: Print container debug output

options.name: --image
options.description: Override default container image

options.name: -e
options.description: Pass environment variable(s) to the container

options.name: -T
options.description: Disable pseudo-tty allocation (useful to get clean stdout)

subcommands.name: share
subcommands.description: Create temporary public url for current project using ngrok

options.name: --host
options.description: Override a hostname for ngrok to route to (default is $VIRTUAL_HOST)

subcommands.name: share-v2
subcommands.description: Create a temporary public URL for the project using Cloudflare Tunnel

subcommands.name: start
subcommands.description: Start tunnel and print public URL

subcommands.name: stop
subcommands.description: Stop tunnel

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Prints tunnel status and public URL (if Active)

subcommands.name: url
subcommands.description: Prints tunnel public URL

subcommands.name: logs
subcommands.description: Prints tunnel container logs

subcommands.name: vhosts
subcommands.description: List all virtual *.docksal hosts registered in Docksal proxy

name: docker
d
subcommands.description: Run Docker commands directly

name: docker-compose
dc
subcommands.description: Run Docker Compose commands directly

name: docker-machine
dm
subcommands.description: Run Docker Machine commands directly

subcommands.name: composer
subcommands.description: Run Composer commands

subcommands.name: drush
subcommands.description: Drush command (requires Drupal)

subcommands.name: drupal
subcommands.description: Drupal Console command (requires Drupal 8)

subcommands.name: platform
subcommands.description: Platform.sh's CLI (requires docksal/cli 2.3+)

subcommands.name: terminus
subcommands.description: Pantheon's Terminus (requires docksal/cli 2.1+)

subcommands.name: wp
subcommands.description: WordPress CLI command (requires WordPress)

subcommands.name: cleanup
subcommands.description: Remove all unused Docker images, unused Docksal volumes and containers

options.name: --images
options.description: Docker images cleanup Wizard

options.name: --hard
options.description: Remove ALL stopped containers even unrelated to Docksal (potentially destructive operation)

subcommands.name: diagnose
subcommands.description: Show diagnostic information for troubleshooting and bug reporting

subcommands.name: sysinfo
subcommands.description: Show system information

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Update Docksal

options.name: DOCKSAL_VERSION
options.description: Update Docksal to the latest development version

name: version
--version
v
-v
subcommands.description: Print fin version. [v, -v] prints short version
